title: Unearthing a Pharaoh's Hidden Treasure
story: Archaeologist Howard Carter struck gold in Egypt's Valley of the Kings. After years of searching, he finally discovered the entrance to Tutankhamun's tomb in 1922. On November 4, Carter's team carefully chipped away at a sealed doorway, revealing a chamber untouched for over 3,000 years. Inside lay a dazzling array of treasures, including the famous golden death mask of the boy pharaoh. This incredible find brought ancient Egyptian history to life, captivating the world's imagination.
activity_question: What did Howard Carter discover in Egypt?
follow_up_question: Why do you think Tutankhamun's tomb remained hidden for so long?

title: Axes for Acres: A Unique Land Deal
story: Imagine trading land for tools! In 1819, Māori Chiefs Hongi Hika and Rewa made an unusual deal on November 4. They sold a huge area of land in Kerikeri, New Zealand to the Church Missionary Society. The price? Not money, but 48 felling axes! This exchange shows how different cultures valued things in the past.
activity_question: What did the Māori Chiefs receive in exchange for their land?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the Māori Chiefs wanted axes instead of money?

title: Art Treasure Trove Opens in Jakarta
story: Jakarta buzzed with excitement as a new art museum flung open its doors in 2017. The Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art in Nusantara showcased amazing pieces from Indonesian tycoon Haryanto Adikoesoemo's collection. On November 4, art lovers flocked to see paintings, sculptures, and more from around the world.
activity_question: What type of museum opened in Jakarta?
activity_choices: Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art
activity_answer: Museum of Modern and Contemporary Art
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for cities to have art museums?
